April is Almora at its most accessible and beautiful.
April is arguably Bhimtal's best month. Temperatures settle at 20-28°C, wildflowers carpet every hillside, and the lake is warm enough for rowing without chattering teeth.
April is Bomdila's showpiece month. Everything aligns — weather, blooms, road conditions, and daylight hours.
April Dirang is the complete package. The valley explodes with color, the Sangti Valley meadows are at their greenest, hot springs feel like a spa day, and every road is open.
April is Landour's Goldilocks month. Warm days (15-23°C), cool evenings, wildflowers everywhere, and tourist numbers still low because Mussoorie's main season hasn't peaked.
April Mawphlang is the sacred forest in full performance — every surface mossy, every branch orchid-laden, every path leading somewhere ancient.
April unlocks Mechuka. The valley that was sealed for months opens to reveal one of Arunachal's most beautiful landscapes — a Tibetan-Buddhist village ringed by snow peaks, bisected by the Siyom…
April in Mussoorie is warm, clear, and increasingly busy.
April is pre-season Nainital at its warmest sweet spot: 20-24°C days, single-digit humidity, and skies so clear you can count Himalayan peaks from Snow View.
May is the month Gurudongmar was made for.
May is Lolab Valley at its absolute peak.
May opens Sissu fully — 5-18°C. The waterfall is in full flow, crashing 100 feet past the village.
May is the final call before the rains seal the mountains.
June brings the first wave of Ladakh tourism, but Alchi — 70km from Leh — stays quieter than Hemis or Thiksey.
June is when Bangus Valley becomes genuinely accessible.
June starts pleasant (14-25°C) before the monsoon's leading edge arrives around June 20th.
June is peak season's opening act. Tour groups start appearing on the Leh-Srinagar highway, but Lamayuru still gets a fraction of Leh's crowds.
June brings peak season warmth (15-28°C) and the Likir monastery festival—Likir Gustor—which typically falls in June or July.
June heats up the valley floor (18-28°C) while keeping the heights cool.
June is Sissu at its most accessible and dramatic — 10-22°C.
July is peak Ladakh season. Tour buses visit Alchi as a day trip from Leh.
July brings the paradox of Ladakh's "monsoon.

July at Likir is warm and lively. The monastery is at peak activity with visiting scholars, the village is green with mature barley, and the golden Maitreya practically glows under intense summer sun.
September is Alchi's autumn grace period.
September is Bangus's autumn farewell. The wildflowers fade, the grass turns gold, and the Gujjar shepherds begin their descent with livestock.
September is the golden month—literally.
September is when the monsoon recedes and Landour begins its spectacular autumn transition.
September brings Likir's harvest season and the beginning of tourist decline.
September brings the monsoon's retreat and Lolab's most dramatic transformation.
September is Sissu's golden turn. The monsoon retreats, skies clear, and the valley starts its autumn transition.
